seoTitle: Devils Creek vs Diamond Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Devils Creek and Diamond Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Devils Creek vs Diamond Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Devils Creek and Diamond Tree.
heading: Devils Creek vs Diamond Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Devils Creek, Western Australia and Diamond Tree, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Devils Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Diamond Tree (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Devils Creek or Diamond Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Devils Creek has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Diamond Tree scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Devils Creek scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Diamond Tree scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Devils Creek or Diamond Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Devils Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
